title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
A woman reveals that a recent codicil to Hubert Litcham Rock's will requires Diana Clifes to marry Mr. Baring to inherit her estate; otherwise, the property passes to her cousin, Harry Dahlhausen. Hercule Poirot uses this information to debunk the theory that Diana murdered her stepfather for the inheritance. He explains that the crime scene was staged to look like a suicide, but the killer made several errors.

Poirot identifies the secretary, Jeffrey Keene (also referred to as Jeffrey King), as the murderer. Poirot explains that Keene killed the victim earlier using a silencer and then staged the body. To create a false timeline, Keene fired a military pistol from a living room window at 8:12 PM to mimic the sound of a gunshot. However, Keene failed to account for Diana being in the garden, which left footprints in the flower bed. While the killer stepped on the flower bed to hide his own tracks, the presence of only four footprints proved someone had wiped them away between Diana's visits to the garden. Furthermore, Keene attempted to hide a bullet he had picked up by claiming it was a silk rosebud. Upon Poirot's accusation, Keene collapses.

Following the resolution, Diana expresses a desire to marry Captain Marshall regardless of the inheritance, and Harry offers to split the estate with her. In a separate scene, Poirot receives a desperate, mysterious phone call from a woman at "Swan Garden" claiming she is in danger and mentioning a table with yellow irises. Upon arriving at Swan Garden, Poirot notes that only one table features yellow irises, while the others have red tulips.
